description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body composed of vertical wooden staves, bound together by two wide, metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns and subtle knots, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The metallic bands are a dull gray, suggesting galvanized steel or iron, and they provide structural reinforcement to the barrel. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top appearing to be tightly sealed. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface is matte with a slightly rough texture. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or as decorative elements in parks and public spaces.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with metal hoops, featuring a sealed wooden top and a matte, weathered finish. The barrel is reinforced with two wide, gray metallic bands and lacks any visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the barrel, with both metal hoops and the sealed wooden top clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with wood grain details prominent.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 1, emphasizing the smooth, continuous wood surface and the matte finish of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view nearly identical to the front, with the same sealed top and visible metal hoops, showing consistent wood texture.
